decision: Use only software-action-test-reliability-agent for verification sign-off.
reason: The implementation work is already reduced to a localized OpenAPI contract patch in backend/docs/openapi.yaml; no further architecture or contract-design branching is required.

decision: Keep market-scoped position routes under the Markets tag and keep /v0/userposition/{marketId} under Users.
reason: server.go wires separate market-scoped and authenticated-user routes; API-ISSUES.md flags market-position routes as ambiguously identified under Users.
impact: Tag ownership now matches implemented route intent without changing public path organization.

decision: Document /v0/privateprofile as legacy JSON success plus plain-text auth/error responses, while documenting envelope responses and explicit 403 password-change requirements only for handlers that now use WAVE04 failure envelopes.
reason: privateuser handler still uses http.Error while the touched profile mutation, bet/sell, and authenticated user-position handlers now use handlers.WriteResult/WriteFailure with ValidateUserAndEnforcePasswordChangeGetUser.
impact: The contract matches current code behavior without inventing extra migration work outside this wave.

decision: Document privateprofile as a legacy non-envelope route and keep the remaining patch limited to implemented WAVE04 envelope/auth behavior.
reason: The task is contract alignment only. Current code shows privateprofile still returns direct JSON on success and plain-text http.Error responses on failures, while the profile mutations, bet/sell, and authenticated user-position route already use the new envelope helpers.
